rules: Domain layer must not import from infrastructure
All external dependencies must be wrapped behind interfaces in ports/
Adapters implement port interfaces and live in adapters/
Application services orchestrate domain logic through ports, never directly through adapters
No framework-specific types (NestJS decorators, BullMQ types, Appwrite SDK types) in the domain layer

versioning: Bump package.json version for all changes

implementation: Prefer dependency injection via NestJS providers; never `new` an adapter inside domain code
All external I/O (X API, Firecrawl, OpenRouter, Appwrite, Redis) goes through a port interface
Validate all external input (HTTP, queue payloads) with zod schemas at the boundary
Use Bun's native crypto and fetch over Node polyfills where possible
Long-running work belongs in a BullMQ processor, never on the request path
review: Flag any direct adapter import inside domain or application code (hexagonal violation)
Flag any third-party SDK type leaking into a port interface
Flag missing zod validation on new HTTP handlers or queue processors
Flag synchronous external I/O on the HTTP request path
Flag tokens, secrets, or PII appearing in logs
documentation: Keep docs/ as the source of truth; update the relevant doc in the same PR as the code change
Cross-link between docs using relative paths so navigation works on GitHub
When adding a new port or adapter, update docs/interfaces.md in the same PR
When changing the Appwrite schema, update docs/data-model.md and scripts/setup-appwrite.ts together
Prefer concise tables over prose for stack/config/field listings
delivery: Use Conventional Commits for PR titles (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, test:, refactor:)
Each PR closes exactly one milestone issue from docs/implementation-plan.md
Never force-push to main; never merge without green CI
PR body must include an Acceptance criteria checklist mirroring the linked issue
Bump package.json version in the same PR as the change (matches versioning rule)
triage: Branch names: feat/<short-slug>, fix/<short-slug>, chore/<short-slug>, docs/<short-slug>
One milestone issue = one branch = one PR; do not bundle milestones
If a triaged task touches a port interface, classify as feat even if the change looks small
If scope creeps beyond the linked milestone issue, stop and split into a new issue rather than expanding
Defer anything in the v1 out-of-scope list in docs/architecture.md — open a follow-up issue, do not implement
